content.full_text:
June 10, 1991

Colleen McGee  
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Technical Assistance Division  
428 "J" Street; Suite 700  
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Lobbyist Registration

Dear Ms. McGee:

This letter is confirm the understanding we reached during our telephone conversation of this morning.

During that conversation you told me that it was not necessary for a lobbying firm to amend its registration adding our firm as a client (on behalf of some of our clients) if that lobbying firm was not compensated (doing the work "pro bono") and if that lobbying firm did not incur any activity expenses on behalf of our clients [reimbursement for ordinary business expenses; i.e., telephone, copies, etc.; would not constitute "compensation"].

If this is contrary to law or to our understanding, please let me know immediately.

Cordially,

Penny Harding

PH:sf
